title: Design LLM eval platform (Braintrust/LangSmith) + DSA review
focus: Slicing dimensions; PII redaction; continuous online eval.

items.label: Min Cost TO Connect All Points

tracks.label: ML System Design · LLM eval platform

items.label: Design an LLM eval platform (Braintrust/LangSmith style)
interviewQuestions: Walk me through designing an internal LLM eval platform.
What dimensions would you slice eval results by?
How would you support pointwise scoring, pairwise comparison, rubric grading, LLM-as-judge, human review, and gold tests in one platform?

items.label: Online + offline eval pipelines
interviewQuestions: How would you continuously evaluate a production LLM agent on real traffic without leaking PII?
How do you keep eval datasets fresh as user behavior shifts — what's the refresh cadence?
How would you collect human evals with rater calibration, gold examples, inter-rater agreement, and quality audits?

items.label: Design autoraters and meta-evaluation for LLM quality
interviewQuestions: How would you train an autorater from pairwise or pointwise human ratings?
Why is pairwise preference data often easier to collect reliably than absolute quality scores?
How would you meta-evaluate an autorater against human judgment using accuracy, Spearman correlation, slices, and drift checks?
What refresh strategy keeps autoraters useful as product models improve and distribution shifts?

items.label: Clarify requirements, users, constraints, and business metric for: Design LLM eval platform (Braintrust/LangSmith)
interviewQuestions: What are the first five clarifying questions before proposing any model?
What is the business metric, what is the ML proxy metric, and where can they diverge?
What scale, latency, freshness, privacy, and reliability constraints change the architecture?

items.label: Defend data, labels, baseline, model choice, feature freshness, and serving path for: Design LLM eval platform (Braintrust/LangSmith)
interviewQuestions: What baseline would you launch before a complex model, and how would you know it is insufficient?
Where do labels come from, what bias do they contain, and how delayed are they?
Which features must be computed online vs offline, and what happens when the feature store is slow?

items.label: Cover monitoring, rollback, cost, security, failure modes, and launch plan for: Design LLM eval platform (Braintrust/LangSmith)
interviewQuestions: What online metrics, guardrails, and cohort slices decide whether this system is safe to ramp?
What is the rollback trigger, fallback experience, and incident owner if quality or latency regresses?
What are the top security, privacy, abuse, or cost risks, and what controls would you ship in v1?

references.label: Chip Huyen — Machine Learning Systems Design
Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer exam guide
Google ML system design mock interview — YouTube recommendations
